channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How Block is accelerating engineering velocity through developer experience
item.description: The Block ecosystem of brands – including Square, Cash App, Spiral and TIDAL – is driven by more than 4,000 engineers and thousands of interconnected software systems. Today, Block is doubling down on engineering velocity, investing in major initiatives to help teams ship software even faster.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump’s crypto reserve is a payoff for loyalists
item.description: President Donald Trump has found a new way to reward his supporters. On Sunday, Trump announced in a Truth Social post that he will issue an executive order creating a “Crypto Strategic Reserve” that will include Bitcoin, Ethereum, and three other cryptocurrencies. “I will make sure the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: Mozilla is under fire for its updated Firefox user agreement
item.description: Mozilla last week updated the Firefox user agreement — something that normally does not provoke strong reactions. This time, however, the changes led to a wave of criticism, because some of the wording can be interpreted as giving Mozilla free rein to do whatever it wants with users’ data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia GTC 2025: What to expect from the AI leader
item.description: No company has both driven and benefited from AI advancements more than Nvidia. Last year, Nvidia’s GTC 2024 grabbed headlines with its introduction of the Blackwell architecture and the DGX systems powered by it. With GTC 2025 right around the corner (it runs March 17- 21 in San Jose, Calif.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: R.I.P. Skype: 2003-2025
item.description: The time is up for Skype, one of the O.G.s of online communication. The once-popular 22-year-old communications software is being retired by Microsoft in May 2025, with the company’s Teams collaboration suite replacing its predecessor.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: East Bay startup’s new stove that plugs into a normal wall outlet could be major gain for health and climate
item.description: The sleek, standard four-burner electric induction stove runs on 120 volts, meaning there is no need to pay a licensed electrician thousands of dollars to rewire to 240 volts, which many electric stoves require.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Tech, life science companies slash hundreds more Bay Area workers
item.description: Latest layoffs affect workers in the South Bay, East Bay and SF, marking a fresh round of employment setbacks for the vital sectors.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: What is retrieval-augmented generation? More accurate and reliable LLMs
item.description: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is a technique used to “ground” large language models (LLMs) with specific data sources, often sources that weren’t included in the models’ original training.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How secure information sharing can be easier, less burdensome
item.description: Bill Anderson, the principal product manager at Mattermost, said agencies need to understand how to remove some of the complexities to improve collaboration. The post How secure information sharing can be easier, less burdensome first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: IBM closes Hashicorp buy, looks to boost enterprise multicloud and AI automation technology
item.description: In announcing the closure of its $6.4 billion buy of Hashicorp, IBM said its goal is to infuse HashiCorp automation and security technology in every data center possible.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Gabbard decries Britain's reported demand for Apple to provide backdoor access to users' cloud data
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard says she has serious concerns about the British government's reported demand that Apple provide backdoor access to any data stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One Tech Tip: Getting a lot of unwanted phone calls? Here are ways to stop them
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it's a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it's enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NFL NextGen Stats team unveils new tool to help fans better understand combine results
item.description: The NFL scouting combine provides a blizzard of data with hundreds of prospects running through various drills ranging from the 40-yard dash to the vertical jump to a short shuttle.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI & Data Exchange 2025: TD Synnex Public Sector’s Trey Bostick on ensuring secure data flow to support AI expansion
item.description: As agencies expand use cases, scaling AI and reaping benefits will hinge on evolving federal data infrastructure, TD Synnex Public Sector expert explains.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: F5 targets AI application security with renewed workload management software
item.description: F5 is evolving its core application and load balancing software to help customers secure and manage AI-powered and multicloud workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI & Data Exchange 2025: Guidehouse’s Tracy Jones on AI readiness and key steps to prepare your organization
item.description: AI readiness must take into account people, technology and processes collectively, explains the associate director of Guidehouse’s AI and data practice.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Vietnam plans energy shift toward building more solar, less reliance on gas and coal
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Vietnam is revising its energy plans to focus more on large solar farms and less on reliance on coal and natural gas. The fast-growing economy now aims to get 16% of its energy from the sun — more than triple its earlier target of just 5%.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What’s next for Microsoft’s Semantic Kernel
item.description: At the heart of Microsoft’s AI application development strategy is Semantic Kernel, an open source set of tools for managing and orchestrating AI prompts.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft’s .NET 10 arrives in first preview
item.description: Microsoft has rolled out the first preview of .NET 10, a major new version of the company’s flagship software development platform. Likely to arrive as a production release in November, .NET 10 introduces C# 14 and adds major enhancements to the .NET runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips, which power artificial intelligence systems, continued to grow, sending the company's stock higher after hours. For the three months that ended Jan.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Man who said he was upset with Musk and Trump charged with threatening to burn down xAI facility
item.description: ASHLAND CITY, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man has been charged with an act of terrorism after he threatened to burn down an xAI facility because he was upset with its founder, Elon Musk, and President Donald Trump, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Critical infrastructure: The rising ransomware victim
item.description: Ferhat Dikbiyik, the chief research and intelligence officer at Black Kite, explains how critical infrastructure providers can better protect their systems. The post Critical infrastructure: The rising ransomware victim first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Autonomous robotaxi successfully completes 75 mph highway testing
item.description: Motional is developing autonomous vehicles capable of handling a wide range of real-world scenarios. Its high-speed robotaxi testing reached speeds of 75 mph.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
